title: ricinus communis var. minor
intro: Ricinus communis var. minor, commonly known as wild castor bean or least castor bean, is a variant of the castor oil plant, characterized by its smaller size and less toxic seeds compared to the typical castor bean plant. The medicinal properties of Ricinus communis var. minor are attributed to its rich content of ricinoleic acid, which has anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce inflammation, and provide relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Ricinus communis var. minor include castor oil, which can be obtained through cold pressing of the seeds, and is often used topically or internally to promote digestive health and reduce inflammation.
